meta_title: Impact Doors in Pembroke Park, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Pembroke Park, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Protect your home today.
h1: Impact Doors in Pembroke Park, FL — Built to Outlast Every Storm Season
intro:
Pembroke Park may sit about five miles from the Atlantic coastline, but don't let that distance lull you into a false sense of security. Broward County's inland communities face the same ferocious wind speeds that batter beachside neighborhoods when a major hurricane tracks across South Florida — and with a designated wind zone rating of 170 mph, Pembroke Park homes are held to strict hurricane-rated product standards under the Florida Building Code. For the roughly 55 percent of homes in Pembroke Park that were built before 1994 — many of which still rely on hollow-core entry doors, aging aluminum sliding glass doors, or wood-frame French doors — the question isn't whether an upgrade is needed, but how soon it can happen. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout South Florida as "The Kings of Service," is here to provide that upgrade.

In neighborhoods like Pembroke Park Estates, homeowners have worked hard to build equity in properties averaging around $270,000. Those investments deserve real protection — not plywood sheets hastily nailed over doors every time a storm watch is issued. Modern impact doors from trusted brands like ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI eliminate that annual scramble entirely. These doors feature laminated safety glass bonded with a tough polyvinyl interlayer, heavy-gauge aluminum or steel frames, and multi-point locking systems that engage at three or more points along the door frame, dramatically reducing the risk of blow-in during high-wind events. They are engineered specifically to meet and exceed the rigorous testing requirements that apply to Broward County's 170-mph wind zone.

Beyond storm protection, the benefits of installing impact doors in Pembroke Park are both immediate and long-lasting. You'll notice the difference the moment your new entry door closes — a solid, airtight seal that cuts down on outside noise from nearby corridors like Hallandale Beach Boulevard, reduces energy loss in South Florida's relentless heat, and adds a polished, modern curb appeal that raises your home's value. Neighbors in Hollywood, West Park, Hallandale Beach, and Miramar have already made the switch in large numbers, and insurance carriers serving Broward County have responded with meaningful premium discounts for homeowners who document their impact-rated openings. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle everything in-house — no subcontractors, no surprises — so Pembroke Park families receive the quality and accountability they deserve from start to finish.

section_why.h2: Why Pembroke Park Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you invite a contractor into your home for a project as significant as replacing entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors, you want to know that the crew showing up has real experience, real credentials, and a real stake in the outcome. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings more than 60 years of combined installation experience to every project across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We have completed more than 500 homes throughout the region, and our team understands the specific demands of Broward County's climate, building codes, and permit requirements inside and out. That means fewer delays, cleaner installations, and zero headaches when it comes time for your Town of Pembroke Park Building Department inspection.

One of the most important things that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from many competitors operating in the Pembroke Park area is our commitment to 100 percent in-house installation. We do not subcontract our labor to third-party crews. Every measurement, every frame preparation, every door installation, and every weather-seal application is performed by our own trained, licensed, and insured technicians. This matters enormously for impact door installations, where precise shimming, proper anchoring into the rough opening, and correct application of flashing and sealant can mean the difference between a door that performs flawlessly in a 170-mph wind event and one that fails under pressure. Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and our growing portfolio of five-star reviews from homeowners across communities like Pembroke Park Estates are testaments to the consistency of that approach.

We also believe that cost should never be the barrier standing between a Pembroke Park family and genuine storm protection. That is why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified buyers. For homeowners in a lower-middle income community like Pembroke Park, where household budgets are carefully managed, the ability to spread the cost of a door upgrade over time — while immediately enjoying lower energy bills, reduced insurance premiums, and lasting peace of mind — makes the decision considerably easier. Our team will walk you through every financing option, every product choice, and every permitting step during your free in-home consultation, so you always know exactly what you are getting and what it will cost.

section_history.h2: Pembroke Park's Storm Legacy: Why Impact Doors Are No Longer Optional
section_history.body:
Pembroke Park has weathered at least nine named storms since 1990, and three of those events left scars that are still part of the community's collective memory. Hurricane Andrew in August 1992 remains the defining storm of modern South Florida history. Though Andrew's core tracked through Homestead and the southern reaches of Miami-Dade County, the storm's outer bands delivered punishing wind gusts to Broward County communities, including Pembroke Park. Homes with inadequate door hardware, outdated sliding glass door frames, and single-pane glazing suffered significant damage, and the aftermath prompted Florida to overhaul its building codes entirely — the first major revision of what would eventually evolve into today's Florida Building Code. If your Pembroke Park home was built before 1994, it predates those code changes, which means its original doors almost certainly do not meet current hurricane protection standards.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 hit Broward County particularly hard, arriving as a fast-moving system that maintained tremendous wind speeds across the entire county. Pembroke Park residents reported widespread door failures — sliding glass doors blown off their tracks, entry doors forced inward, and French door sets that buckled under the sustained wind load. Wind-borne debris traveling at highway speeds turned ordinary projectiles into door-penetrating missiles, and homes without impact-rated glazing experienced interior damage that was far more costly than the door replacement itself would have been. Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a reminder that no hurricane season is truly quiet for South Florida. Even homes that had begun upgrading windows still had vulnerable door openings, and those gaps in protection proved costly for families throughout the Pembroke Park area. The lesson from each of these storms is the same: every exterior door opening in your home needs to be protected with a properly rated impact door — not shutters, not hope, and certainly not the original door that came with a pre-1994 home.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Pembroke Park and Broward County
section_code.body:
Understanding the regulatory landscape for impact door installations in Pembroke Park is essential before any project begins. Pembroke Park falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition, and while the community is not classified as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved primarily for Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closer to the coast — it still sits within a wind speed zone requiring products rated to withstand at least 170 mph. This means that any impact door installed in Pembroke Park must carry appropriate product approval documentation, typically a Florida Product Approval (FL number) issued through the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation, demonstrating that the door system has passed the large missile impact test, cyclic pressure testing, and structural load requirements specified by the FBC and ASTM standards.

All impact door installations in Pembroke Park require a permit issued through the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department. This is not optional, and working with an unlicensed contractor who skips the permit process can result in fines, required removal of the installed product, and complications when you attempt to sell or refinance your home. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from submitting the application and product approval documentation to scheduling the required inspections and obtaining the final sign-off. Our team has established working relationships with local building departments throughout Broward County, and we understand exactly what Pembroke Park inspectors need to see to approve an impact door installation quickly and without re-inspection.

It is also worth noting that the FBC requires impact doors to be installed per the manufacturer's approved installation instructions, which are specific to the product approval number. Deviations — even seemingly minor ones like using the wrong anchor type or spacing — can void both the product approval and the manufacturer's warranty. Our in-house installation crews are trained on the specific requirements for every ESW and CWI door system we install, ensuring full compliance on every Pembroke Park project.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Engineered for Pembroke Park's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact door systems from two of the most respected names in the South Florida hurricane protection industry: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers design and test their products specifically for the demands of Florida's coastal and near-coastal wind zones, and both carry Florida Product Approvals that meet the requirements applicable to Pembroke Park's 170-mph wind zone designation. Whether you are replacing a worn-out entry door, upgrading a sliding glass door that opens to your backyard patio, or installing a set of elegant French doors between your interior spaces and a rear lanai, ESW and CWI offer product lines that combine certified storm performance with the aesthetics Pembroke Park homeowners expect.

ESW impact entry doors and sliding glass door systems are built around heavy-duty extruded aluminum frames with thermally broken profiles that reduce heat transfer — an important feature in a region where air conditioning runs virtually year-round. Their laminated glass packages are bonded with a high-strength interlayer that holds the glass together upon impact, preventing the catastrophic blow-through that can depressurize a home and trigger roof failure during a hurricane. ESW's multi-point locking systems engage at the top, bottom, and center of the door frame simultaneously, creating a fortified seal that standard single-point locks simply cannot match. CWI door systems offer comparable performance with a slightly different product profile, including options well-suited to homes in Pembroke Park Estates where architectural character and curb appeal are important considerations for homeowners protecting and growing their equity.

Both brands offer a range of finish options, glass tint selections, and hardware styles, so your impact door upgrade doesn't have to look utilitarian. Low-E glass coatings are available across both product lines, helping Pembroke Park homeowners reduce solar heat gain and trim monthly FPL energy costs at the same time. Our team will guide you through the full product selection process during your free consultation, matching the right ESW or CWI system to your home's specific opening dimensions, architectural style, and budget.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Pembroke Park — And How to Finance Them
section_cost.body:
For Pembroke Park homeowners with properties averaging around $270,000, understanding the true cost — and true value — of an impact door installation is an important part of the decision-making process. The cost of impact doors varies depending on the type of door (entry, sliding glass, or French), the size of the opening, the specific ESW or CWI product selected, and the complexity of the installation. As a general benchmark, a single impact-rated entry door installation in Pembroke Park typically ranges from roughly $1,500 to $3,500 installed, while larger sliding glass door systems or multi-panel French door sets can range from $3,000 to $7,500 or more depending on width and configuration. These are investments, not expenses — and the return begins immediately.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes that investment accessible through flexible financing options designed specifically for South Florida homeowners. Qualified buyers can get started with payments as low as $87 per month and $0 down, spreading the cost of their door upgrade over a manageable term without draining emergency savings or delaying the protection their family needs. For a Pembroke Park household managing a budget carefully, the ability to begin saving on energy bills and insurance premiums right away — while making predictable monthly payments — makes the math work in your favor from day one. Our team will help you explore every available financing option during your consultation and connect you with the program that best fits your financial situation.

From a pure return-on-investment perspective, impact doors in Pembroke Park deliver on multiple fronts simultaneously. Homeowners across Broward County routinely report wind mitigation insurance discounts of 10 to 30 percent or more on their annual premiums after documenting impact-rated openings — savings that can amount to hundreds of dollars per year. Reduced energy costs from thermally improved door systems add further savings month after month. And perhaps most importantly, the protection impact doors provide against storm damage can prevent tens of thousands of dollars in interior repairs that a single major hurricane can inflict on a home with inadequate door openings.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Pembroke Park Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial reasons to upgrade to impact doors in Pembroke Park is the insurance premium savings that follow. Florida homeowners insurance has become increasingly expensive in recent years as carriers have responded to mounting storm losses throughout the state. Broward County policyholders have felt this pressure acutely, with annual premiums rising sharply even for homes that have not filed claims. However, homeowners who document certified hurricane protection products — including impact-rated doors — through a licensed wind mitigation inspection are entitled to premium credits that can meaningfully offset those increases. For Pembroke Park homeowners, those credits can translate to savings of anywhere from a few hundred to over a thousand dollars per year depending on the policy, the carrier, and the scope of the protection documented.

The wind mitigation inspection process, governed by the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation and typically conducted by a licensed home inspector or engineer, evaluates multiple attributes of your home's storm protection — including opening protection for all exterior doors and windows. When an inspector documents that your home's doors are protected with impact-rated systems carrying valid Florida Product Approvals, that information is submitted to your insurance carrier on a standardized wind mitigation report (OIR-B1-1802). Most major carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — are required by Florida law to apply actuarially justified credits based on that report. The more openings you protect, the greater the credits tend to be.

Beyond the annual premium savings, there is the broader financial protection that impact doors provide against the out-of-pocket costs that come with storm damage. A deductible on a Florida homeowners policy for hurricane damage is typically 2 to 5 percent of the insured value of the home — meaning a Pembroke Park homeowner insured at $270,000 could face a $5,400 to $13,500 deductible before insurance even kicks in after a major storm. Preventing interior damage by maintaining the integrity of your door openings during a hurricane is the most direct way to avoid ever having to write that check.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Pembroke Park if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Pembroke Park sits within a 170-mph wind zone under the Florida Building Code, meaning storm-force winds reaching your neighborhood can be just as destructive as those hitting beachfront properties. Past storms like Hurricane Wilma and Hurricane Irma caused significant door damage throughout inland Broward County communities. Distance from the coast provides no meaningful reduction in hurricane-force wind exposure during a direct or near-direct hit.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Pembroke Park, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Pembroke Park typically range from around $1,500 for a single entry door to $7,500 or more for large sliding glass or French door systems, depending on the product, size, and installation complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down for qualified buyers, making it easy to upgrade your home's protection without a large upfront payment. Contact us for a free, no-obligation quote specific to your home's openings.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact doors in Pembroke Park?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door installations in Pembroke Park require a permit issued by the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department. The permit process involves submitting the door's Florida Product Approval documentation and passing a post-installation inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process for you — from application to final inspection — ensuring your installation is fully compliant with Broward County's Florida Building Code requirements and properly documented for insurance and resale purposes.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in Pembroke Park?
faqs.a: Most residential impact door installations in Pembroke Park are completed in a single day. A straightforward entry door replacement typically takes two to four hours, while a sliding glass door or multi-panel French door installation may take a full day depending on the opening size and site conditions. The permitting process, which Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages on your behalf, typically adds one to three weeks before installation can begin. We will keep you informed of the timeline every step of the way.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowners insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires homeowners insurance carriers to apply wind mitigation credits when a licensed inspector documents that your home's exterior openings — including doors — are protected with impact-rated products carrying valid Florida Product Approvals. Pembroke Park homeowners commonly see annual premium reductions ranging from 10 to 30 percent or more after a wind mitigation inspection documents their impact door upgrades. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can recommend qualified inspectors in the Broward County area to help you capture those savings.
